questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Twinkling of stars is due to
options: reflection of starlight from the oceanic surface
questions.solution:
The phenomenon of twinkling of stars is due to the atmospheric refraction of starlight.

When starlight enters the Earth's atmosphere, it passes through layers of air at different temperatures and densities. As light travels through these varying layers, it is continuously refracted, or bent, in different directions. This refraction causes the light of the star to fluctuate in intensity and position as observed from Earth, leading to the perception of the star "twinkling." This effect is more pronounced for stars near the horizon compared to those directly overhead because the light path through the atmosphere is longer and passes through more layers of varying density.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Physical Quantity) List II (SI Unit)
A. Temperature  1. Kelvin
B. Weight 2. Kilogram
C. Mass  3. Pascal
D. Pressure  4. Newton

code:
questions.solution:
A. Temperature - The standard unit for temperature in the SI system is Kelvin. ($$K$$)

B. Weight - Weight is actually a measure of the force of gravity acting on an object. The SI unit for force is the Newton. ($$N$$)

C. Mass - The fundamental SI unit for mass is the Kilogram. ($$kg$$)

D. Pressure - Pressure is force per unit area, and its SI unit is the Pascal. ($$Pa$$)

So, the correct matches are:

A - 1

B - 4

C - 2

D - 3

Looking at the options you provided, this combination matches Option 3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The composition of gases in exosphere is
questions.solution:
The exosphere is primarily composed of light gases. Helium and hydrogen are the two main constituents found in this outermost layer of Earth's atmosphere.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: Helium and Hydrogen.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the block P as shown in the figure below were to be at rest, what should the magnitude of force F be?
questions.solution:
Since the block is at rest. Therefore

The sum of force applied on both sides will be equal.

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 10+15=2+15+F \\\\ & \Rightarrow 25=17+F \\\\ & \Rightarrow F=25-17 \\\\ & \therefore F=\mathbf{8} \mathbf{N} \end{aligned} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Sita, 1.5 m high, stands before a plane mirror fixed on a wall to view her full image. What should be the minimum height of the plane mirror so that Sita can view her image fully?
questions.solution:
To determine the minimum height of the plane mirror required for Sita to view her full image, we need to understand the properties of a plane mirror. A plane mirror reflects light in such a way that the image formed is virtual, upright, and of the same size as the object. In order for her to see her complete image, the length of the mirror must be half the height of Sita.

Here's why: When Sita looks at her reflection in the mirror, the topmost and bottommost rays from her body will reflect off the mirror at equal angles. To capture both her head and feet in the reflection, the mirror needs to be at least half her height. This is because each point on Sita's body is reflected in the corresponding point on the mirror at half the height, allowing the complete image to fit.

So, if Sita's height is 1.5 m, the minimum height of the mirror should be:

$$\frac{1.5 \, \text{m}}{2} = 0.75 \, \text{m}$$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 0.75 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements with reference to L-Wave or Long Wave generated by an earthquake is/are correct?1.They follow the Earth's circumference
2.They travel at more or less at a constant rate
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Statement 1 is correct and Statement 2 is not.

L-waves (surface waves) travel along the Earth’s surface (i.e. its circumference) but are dispersive, so their speed varies with frequency.

Answer: 1 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The presence of magnetic field can be determined using which one of the following instruments?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Magnetic needle. Here's why:

A magnetic needle, often found in a compass, is specifically designed to align itself with the magnetic field lines of the Earth. This alignment is the basis of how compasses work.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: Ammeter - An ammeter measures the flow of electric current, not magnetic fields.
  • Option B: Voltmeter - A voltmeter measures electrical potential difference, not magnetic fields.
  • Option D: Motor - A motor uses a magnetic field to produce rotation, but it doesn't directly measure the presence of a magnetic field.

In summary, a magnetic needle is the most direct way to detect the presence of a magnetic field because it physically responds to the field's influence.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true?
options: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon is greater than the force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth.
The force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth is greater than the force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude and are in the same direction.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.
questions.solution:
To determine the correct answer, we need to apply Newton's Third Law of Motion. Newton's Third Law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This means the forces two objects exert on each other are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction.

In the case of the Earth and the Moon, the gravitational force that the Earth exerts on the Moon is equal in magnitude to the gravitational force that the Moon exerts on the Earth. However, these forces are in opposite directions.

Mathematically, the force due to gravity between two masses, $m_1$ (Earth) and $m_2$ (Moon), is given by Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation:

$$ F = G \frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2} $$

where $G$ is the gravitational constant, and $r$ is the distance between the centers of the two masses.

Since the masses and the distance between the centers remain the same, the magnitude of the force is the same for both the Earth and the Moon. Hence, by Newton's Third Law, we know that these forces are also equal in magnitude and opposite in direction.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For a material under static conditions, which one among the following statements is correct ?
options: Both perfect conductor and insulator can be charged
Both perfect conductor and insulator cannot be charged
questions.solution:
Static conditions mean that electric charges are not moving. They stay still.

In an insulator, charges cannot move freely. They stay in one place where they are put.

This means an insulator can keep an electric charge when conditions are static.

In a conductor, charges can move around easily. This makes it hard to keep the charges in one spot.

⇒ Under static conditions, an insulator can be charged.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Two conducting wires of the same material and of equal lengths and equal diameters are first connected in parallel and then in series in a circuit across the same potential difference. The ratio of heat produced in parallel and series combinations is
questions.solution:
Let the resistance of each wire be $R$.

Since both wires are identical:

  • In series, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_s = R + R = 2R $$

  • In parallel, equivalent resistance:

$$ R_p = \frac{R \cdot R}{R + R} = \frac{R}{2} $$

Now, heat produced in a circuit is given by Joule’s law:

$$ H = \frac{V^2 t}{R} $$

(where $V$ and $t$ are same in both cases)

So,

For parallel combination:

$$ H_p = \frac{V^2 t}{R_p} = \frac{V^2 t}{R/2} = \frac{2V^2 t}{R} $$

For series combination:

$$ H_s = \frac{V^2 t}{R_s} = \frac{V^2 t}{2R} $$

Now, ratio:

$$ \frac{H_p}{H_s} = \frac{2V^2 t / R}{V^2 t / (2R)} = 4 $$

So, the ratio is:

$$ 4:1 $$

Correct option: Option 2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A stone is thrown horizontally from the top of a 20 m high building with a speed of 12 m/s. It hits the ground at a distance R from the building. Taking g = 10 m/s<sup>2</sup> and neglecting air resistance will give :
questions.solution:
1. Vertical motion

  • Initial vertical velocity $u_y = 0$

  • Height $h = 20\,$m

  • Acceleration $g = 10\,$m/s²

  • Use $$h = \tfrac12\,g\,t^2$$

    so $$20 = \tfrac12\cdot10\cdot t^2 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t^2 = 4 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad t = 2\ \text{s}$$

  1. Horizontal motion

  • Horizontal speed $v_x = 12\,$m/s (constant)

  • Range $R = v_x \,t = 12 \times 2 = 24\,$m

Answer: R = 24 m (Option C)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following sketches correctly describes a lever of second class?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Lever of Second Class

A lever of the second class has three main parts:

  • The fulcrum is at one end. The fulcrum is the point where the lever pivots or turns.
  • The load (the thing you want to move or lift) is in the middle.
  • The effort (where you push or pull) is at the other end, opposite the fulcrum.

In a second-class lever:

  • The load is always between the fulcrum and where you apply effort.
  • The arm where you apply effort (effort arm) is always longer than the part holding the load (load arm), so you get a mechanical advantage greater than 1. This means it's easier to lift something.
  • Examples of second-class levers are wheelbarrows, nutcrackers, and bottle openers.

EXPLANATION:

When you look at sketches of different levers:

  • A second-class lever should always have:
    • Fulcrum at one end,
    • Load in the middle,
    • And effort at the other end.
  • The correct sketch must show the load between the fulcrum and the effort.

So, the sketch that correctly shows a lever of the second class is Option 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following conclusions could not be derived from Rutherford's α-particle scattering experiment?
options: Most of the space in the atom is empty
The radius of the atom is about 10<sup>5</sup> times the radius of the nucleus
Electrons move in a circular path of fixed energy called orbits
Nearby all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus
questions.solution:
Option C cannot be derived from Rutherford's $\alpha$-particle scattering experiment.

Rutherford's experiment led to several conclusions about the structure of the atom:

Option A: Most of the space in the atom is empty. This conclusion was drawn because most of the $\alpha$-particles passed through the gold foil without any deflection, indicating that atoms are mostly empty space.

Option B: The radius of the atom is about $10^5$ times the radius of the nucleus. This was inferred from the fact that very few $\alpha$-particles were deflected back, suggesting a tiny, dense nucleus compared to the overall size of the atom.

Option D: Nearly all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus. This conclusion was reached because the deflection of some $\alpha$-particles implied a very dense center, which contains most of the atomic mass.

Option C refers to the description of electron orbits, which was developed later by Niels Bohr. Bohr's model introduced the concept of electrons in specific orbits with fixed energies, an idea not covered or implied by Rutherford's scattering experiment.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If three resistors of 1 Ohm each connect in parallel to each other the resultant resistance is
questions.solution:
For resistors in parallel, the reciprocal of the total resistance is the sum of reciprocals:

$$ \frac1{R_{\rm eq}} = \frac1{R_1} + \frac1{R_2} + \frac1{R_3} = \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} = 3 $$

So

$$ R_{\rm eq} = \frac1{3}\,\Omega. $$

That corresponds to Option 2: 1/3 Ohm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For an electric circuit given below, the correct combination of voltage (V) and current (I) is
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Parallel Circuits and Ohm’s Law

When resistors are in parallel, the voltage is the same across all resistors.

To find the total current, first combine all resistors into a single "equivalent resistance." Then use Ohm’s Law: V = IR or I = V / R.

EXPLANATION:

Step 1: Find Equivalent Resistance

The resistors, 100 Ω, 200 Ω, and 300 Ω, are all connected in parallel.

The formula for parallel resistors: 1/Req = 1/100 + 1/200 + 1/300

Let’s add:
1/100 = 6/600
1/200 = 3/600
1/300 = 2/600

Add together: (6 + 3 + 2) / 600 = 11 / 600

So, Req = 600 / 11 ≈ 54.55 Ω

Step 2: Find the Voltage

Ohm’s Law says V = IR.

From the circuit, the current through the 100 Ω resistor is given as 3 A.

So, V = 100 × 3 = 300 V

Step 3: Find the Total Current

Now, use the voltage (300 V) to find the current through each resistor:

  • For 100 Ω: I = 300 / 100 = 3 A
  • For 200 Ω: I = 300 / 200 = 1.5 A
  • For 300 Ω: I = 300 / 300 = 1 A

Total current = 3 + 1.5 + 1 = 5.5 A

So, the correct answer is: Option 2) V = 300 V ; I = 5.5 A

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
An electric circuit is given below. $V_1 = 1 V$ and Resistance $R = 1000 \Omega$.

The current through the resistance $R$ is very close to $1$ mA and the voltage across point A and B, $V_{AB} = 1 V$. Now the circuit is changed to :

where value of $V_2 = 5 V$. The internal resistances of both the batteries are $0.1 \Omega$. The current through the resistance $R$ is about :
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} \text { Equivalent voltage } & =\frac{\varepsilon_1 r_1+\varepsilon_2 1_2}{r_1+r_2} \\\\ & =\frac{1 \times 0.1+5 \times 0.1}{0.1+0.1} \\\\ \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{eq}} & =\frac{0.6}{0.2}=3 \mathrm{~V}\end{aligned}$

From first circuit, we have

$$ i=\frac{\varepsilon}{R+r} $$

Value of $r$ is very less can be neglected as compared to $\mathrm{R}$

Now,

$$ \begin{aligned} i & =\frac{\varepsilon}{R} \\\\ 10^{-3} & =\frac{1}{R} \\\\ \mathrm{R} & =10^3=1000 \mathrm{ohm} \end{aligned} $$

Now, from second circuit

$$ \begin{aligned} i_1 & =\frac{\mathrm{E}_{e q}}{\mathrm{R}+r_{e q}}=\frac{3}{1000} \\\\ i_1 & =3 \times 10^{-3}=3 \mathrm{~mA} \end{aligned} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Point of Origin of Earthquake Wave is known as
questions.solution:
The point of origin of an earthquake wave is known as the Focus (Option B).

In seismology, the focus of an earthquake refers to the location within the Earth where the rupture of the fault begins, and seismic waves are initially generated. It is beneath the surface of the Earth. The point directly above the focus, on the Earth's surface, is called the epicenter.

Focus: The exact point inside the Earth where an earthquake originates.

Epicenter: The point on the Earth's surface located directly above the focus.

These concepts are fundamental in understanding how energy is released during an earthquake and how it propagates through the Earth's crust.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following scientists mentioned that an electron in an atom could revolve in certain stable orbits without the emission of radiant energy?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Niels Bohr.

Niels Bohr was a pioneering physicist who made fundamental contributions to our understanding of atomic structure and quantum mechanics. In 1913, Bohr introduced his model of the atom, which incorporated some aspects of classical physics and early quantum theories. One of the key postulates of the Bohr model is that electrons can revolve around the nucleus of an atom in specific, quantized orbits without the emission of radiant energy. These stable orbits correspond to certain fixed energy levels.

Bohr proposed that while the electron remains in one of these discrete energy levels, no energy is radiated. Radiant energy is emitted or absorbed only when an electron transitions from one energy level to another. This idea was revolutionary because, according to classical electromagnetic theory, an accelerating charge such as an electron in orbit should continuously emit radiation and lose energy, leading to a collapse of the orbit. Bohr's model provided a way to circumvent this problem by introducing quantum concepts.

Bohr's theory can be summarized by the equation for the quantized angular momentum of an electron:

$$L = n \hbar$$

where:

  • $$L$$ is the angular momentum
  • $$n$$ is a positive integer (quantum number)
  • $$\hbar$$ is the reduced Planck's constant ( $$\hbar = \frac{h}{2 \pi}$$ )

This discovery was critical in the development of quantum mechanics and helped to explain phenomena that could not be accounted for by classical physics alone. It laid the groundwork for future atomic models and a more comprehensive understanding of atomic and subatomic processes.

In conclusion, Niels Bohr's concept of electrons revolving in quantized stable orbits without radiating energy was fundamental in explaining atomic stability and the discrete nature of atomic spectra.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A current of 0.6 A is drawn by an electric bulb for 10 minutes. Which one of the following is the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit?
questions.solution:
To determine the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit, we can use the relationship between current, charge, and time. The equation we will use is:

$$Q = I \cdot t$$

where:

  • $ Q $ is the electric charge in coulombs (C).
  • $ I $ is the current in amperes (A).
  • $ t $ is the time in seconds (s).

Given:

  • Current $ I = 0.6 $ A
  • Time $ t = 10 $ minutes

First, we need to convert the time from minutes to seconds:

$$ t = 10 \text{ minutes} \times 60 \text{ seconds/minute} = 600 \text{ seconds} $$

Now we can substitute $ I $ and $ t $ into the equation:

$$ Q = 0.6 \text{ A} \times 600 \text{ s} = 360 \text{ C} $$

Therefore, the amount of electric charge that flows through the circuit is 360 C. Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: 360 C

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the total resistance in the following circuit element?
questions.solution:
To find the total resistance in the given circuit, we first need to understand the configuration of the resistors. The image shows three resistors arranged in a combination of series and parallel.

Let's denote the resistances as follows:

  • Two resistors in series with each resistance $$R$$
  • One resistor in parallel with the series combination mentioned above with resistance $$R$$

First, we calculate the equivalent resistance of the two resistors in series:

$$R_{series} = R + R = 2R$$

Next, we consider the parallel combination of this series resistance with the third resistor:

The total resistance for resistors in parallel, where one resistor has resistance $$R$$ and the other has resistance $$2R$$, is given by the formula:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{1}{R} + \frac{1}{2R}$$

Combining the two fractions, we get:

$$\frac{1}{R_{total}} = \frac{2}{2R} + \frac{1}{2R} = \frac{3}{2R}$$

Taking the reciprocal to find the total resistance:

$$R_{total} = \frac{2R}{3}$$

Therefore, the total resistance in the circuit is $$\frac{2R}{3}$$.

So the correct option is:

Option D $$\frac{2R}{3}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: For a human eye, where u is the distance of an object from the eye, f is the focal length of the lens and v is the distance of image from the eye, which is the correct schematic graph?
questions.solution: When object distance increase, in order to maintain image at retina, focal length increases initially, later for extreme distance it becomes constant. Hence, option (c) is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The heating element in an electric iron is usually made of
questions.solution:
The heating element in an electric iron is typically made of a material that has a high resistance to generate sufficient heat when electric current passes through it. Among the options provided, the best choice for this application is Nichrome, which is a nickel-chromium alloy. Nichrome is preferred because it has a high melting point, excellent resistance to oxidation, and stable resistance over a wide range of temperatures.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C Nichrome

To elaborate more, when electric current flows through Nichrome, it encounters resistance, converting electrical energy into heat energy. This principle of Joule heating is utilized in various heating applications, such as in electric irons, toasters, and hair dryers. The equation governing this process is given by:

$$Q = I^2 R t$$

where:

  • $$Q$$ is the heat energy produced in joules
  • $$I$$ is the current in amperes
  • $$R$$ is the resistance in ohms
  • $$t$$ is the time in seconds

Using Nichrome allows manufacturers to create reliable and efficient heating elements that can withstand high temperatures and prolonged usage.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following has valency of 3?
questions.solution:
Valency is the number of electrons an atom needs to gain, lose or share to complete its valence shell. For the given elements:

Carbon

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^2$$

– Valence electrons: 4 → Valency = 4

Oxygen

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^4$$

– Valence electrons: 6 → Valency = 2

Nitrogen

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^3$$

– Valence electrons: 5 → Needs 3 more → Valency = 3

Sodium

– Electron configuration: $$1s^2\,2s^2\,2p^6\,3s^1$$

– Valence electron: 1 → Valency = 1

Answer: Nitrogen (Option 3) has a valency of 3.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: <!-- x-tinymce/html -->Which one among the following is an example of chemical change?
questions.solution:
Mixing NaOH and HCl is a chemical change. In that reaction new substances form (salt and water):

$$\text{NaOH} + \text{HCl} \;\longrightarrow\; \text{NaCl} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$$

Quick check of the other options:

• Sublimation of camphor – physical (phase change)

• Melting of ice – physical (phase change)

• Heating an iron rod – physical (thermal expansion/colour change)

So the correct answer is Option 4.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Copper sulphate crystals available in the market are blue coloured crystals. By careful heating, they turn to white colour. Which one of the following is responsible for the blue colour?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Water. Here's why:

Copper Sulfate Pentahydrate (CuSO₄·5H₂O): The blue crystals you see are actually copper sulfate pentahydrate. This means each copper sulfate molecule (CuSO₄) is bonded to five water molecules. These water molecules are responsible for the blue color.

Dehydration: When you heat the crystals, the water molecules are driven off (dehydration). The remaining copper sulfate (CuSO₄) is white.

The chemical equation for this process is:

$$CuSO₄·5H₂O \xrightarrow{heat} CuSO₄ + 5H₂O$$

Let me know if you have any more questions!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: At which temperature does liquid water show maximum density?
questions.solution:
Liquid water reaches its maximum density at about 4 °C, which is 277 K.

• Answer: Option 2 (277 K)

• Reason: Below 4 °C, the increasing prominence of hydrogen-bond–driven open structures makes water expand, so its density peaks right at 4 °C.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Consider the following reaction:

Fe2O3(s) + 2Al(s) → 2Fe(s) + Al2O3(s)

Which of the following statements about the given reaction is NOT correct?
options: It is an example of displacement reaction
Fe2O3 acts as oxidizing agent
This reaction is applicable to joining of railway tracks
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the given reaction:

$$\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3(\text{s}) + 2\text{Al}(\text{s}) \rightarrow 2\text{Fe}(\text{s}) + \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3(\text{s})$$

Now, let's evaluate each of the given statements:

Option A: It is an example of a displacement reaction.

This is a true statement. In this reaction, aluminum (Al) displaces iron (Fe) from iron(III) oxide (Fe2O3), forming aluminum oxide (Al2O3) and iron (Fe). This is a characteristic of a displacement reaction.

Option B: It is a highly endothermic reaction.

This statement is incorrect. The reaction between iron(III) oxide and aluminum is actually highly exothermic. It releases a large amount of heat, enough to melt iron. This is why it is used in thermite welding, which is utilized to join railway tracks.

Option C: Fe2O3 acts as an oxidizing agent.

This is a true statement. In this reaction, Fe2O3 is reduced to Fe, and in the process, it oxidizes aluminum (Al) to Al2O3. Thus, Fe2O3 acts as an oxidizing agent.

Option D: This reaction is applicable to the joining of railway tracks.

This statement is true. The highly exothermic nature of the reaction makes it suitable for welding railway tracks together in a process known as thermite welding.

Therefore, the statement that is NOT correct is:

Option B: It is a highly endothermic reaction.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which of the following anions has a valency of -3 ?
questions.solution:
1. Valency of an anion = magnitude of its charge.

  2. Write the formula and charge for each:

    a. Nitride: $N^{3-}$ ⇒ valency = 3 (charge = –3)

    b. Nitrate: $NO_{3}^{-}$ ⇒ valency = 1 (charge = –1)

    c. Sulphide: $S^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

    d. Sulphate: $SO_{4}^{2-}$ ⇒ valency = 2 (charge = –2)

  3. The only anion with valency 3 (charge –3) is nitride.

Answer: Option 1 (Nitride).

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals does NOT react with cold water to liberate hydrogen gas?
questions.solution:
To answer the question, let's examine the reactivity of each metal with cold water.

Potassium (Option A): Potassium is a very reactive metal. When it comes into contact with cold water, it reacts vigorously to form potassium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. The reaction is represented by:

$$ 2K + 2H_2O \rightarrow 2KOH + H_2 \uparrow $$

Sodium (Option D): Sodium is also highly reactive with cold water. The reaction produces sodium hydroxide and hydrogen gas. The chemical equation for this reaction is:

$$ 2Na + 2H_2O \rightarrow 2NaOH + H_2 \uparrow $$

Calcium (Option C): Calcium reacts with cold water, though less vigorously than potassium and sodium. The reaction produces calcium hydroxide and hydrogen gas:

$$ Ca + 2H_2O \rightarrow Ca(OH)_2 + H_2 \uparrow $$

Iron (Option B): Iron does not react with cold water to any significant extent to liberate hydrogen gas. The reaction between iron and water typically requires higher temperatures or the presence of steam.

Therefore, the metal that does NOT react with cold water to liberate hydrogen gas is:

Option B: Iron

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Soap with water forms :
questions.solution:
When soap is mixed with water, it forms a specific type of liquid crystal known as a lyotropic liquid crystal. This occurs because the soap molecules, which are amphiphilic (having both hydrophilic and hydrophobic parts), align themselves in water to form various ordered structures like micelles and liquid crystalline phases. The formation and ordering of these structures depend on the concentration of the soap in water and the temperature.

Lyotropic liquid crystals are distinguished by their dependence on the concentration of the solute (in this case, soap) in a solvent (water), unlike thermotropic liquid crystals, which depend primarily on temperature.

Therefore, the correct answer is :

Option D : Lyotropic liquid crystal

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following salts is responsible for temporary hardness of water ?
questions.solution:
Temporary hardness of water is caused by the bicarbonates of calcium and magnesium.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 3: Bicarbonates of magnesium

Reason:

  • Temporary hardness is due to soluble bicarbonates.

  • Permanent hardness is due to chlorides and sulphates of calcium and magnesium.

Therefore, magnesium bicarbonate is responsible for temporary hardness of water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one among the following statements with reference to the properties of water is not correct?
options: The specific heat of water is abnormally high.
Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.
Density of water is higher than ice.
Pure water is a non-conductor of electricity.
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement to determine which one is not correct regarding the properties of water:

Option A: The specific heat of water is abnormally high.

This statement is correct. Water has a very high specific heat capacity, meaning it requires a lot of energy to change its temperature. This property helps to stabilize the climate and is crucial for maintaining life.

Option B: Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.

This statement is not correct. The latent heat of fusion of water is actually quite high. Latent heat of fusion is the amount of heat required to convert a unit mass of a solid into a liquid without a temperature change. For ice, it is 334 kJ/kg, which is relatively high compared to many other substances.

Option C: Density of water is higher than ice.

This statement is correct. The density of liquid water is higher than that of ice. This is why ice floats on water. The unique structure of ice creates more open spaces between molecules, reducing its density.

Option D: Pure water is a non-conductor of electricity.

This statement is correct to an extent. Pure water (distilled water) is a poor conductor of electricity because it lacks ions. However, even small amounts of impurities can make water conductive.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is:

Option B: Latent heat of fusion of water is very low.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one: of the following is considered to be the purest source of water ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 4: Rain water}$$

Reason:

Rain water is considered the purest natural source of water because it is formed by the evaporation of water from rivers, lakes, and seas. During evaporation, most impurities and salts are left behind.

So, among the given options, the purest source of water is:

$$\text{Rain water}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Airbags work on the principle of a chemical reaction triggered by the impact producing a gaseous product that causes a sudden volume change. Which one among the following chemical conversions is responsible for this?
options: Solid carbon dioxide into gaseous carbon dioxide
Sudden conversion of gaseous carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option A: Sodium azide into nitrogen gas

Airbags typically use the rapid decomposition of sodium azide (NaN₃) to produce nitrogen gas (N₂), which inflates the airbag. The chemical reaction involved is:

$$ 2 \text{NaN}_3 \rightarrow 2 \text{Na} + 3 \text{N}_2 \uparrow $$

This reaction produces a large volume of nitrogen gas very quickly, causing the airbag to inflate.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is correct?
options: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites
Amphiboles form about 20% of the Earth's crust
The word 'sedimentary' is derived from the Latin word kelimentum
questions.solution:
The correct statement is Option 1: Pyroxene is commonly found in meteorites.

• Many stony (chondritic) meteorites are dominated by silicate minerals—primarily olivine and pyroxene.

• Amphiboles make up only a few percent (not ~20%) of the Earth’s crust minerals.

• Quartz is essentially insoluble in pure water.

• “Sedimentary” comes from the Latin sedimentum (“a settling”), not “kelimentum.”

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is an endothermic reaction ?
questions.solution:
The endothermic reaction is:

Option 4

$$ 2Pb(NO_3)_2(s) \rightarrow 2PbO(s) + 4NO_2(g) + O_2(g) $$

Reason:

  • Endothermic reaction is a reaction in which heat is absorbed.

  • Thermal decomposition reactions usually need heat to break down a compound.

  • Lead nitrate, $Pb(NO_3)_2$, decomposes on heating, so this is an endothermic reaction.

Why others are not endothermic:

  • Option 1: Combustion of methane is exothermic.

  • Option 2: Respiration/glucose oxidation is exothermic.

  • Option 3: Formation of ammonia is also exothermic.

Correct answer:

Option 4

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following allotropes of carbon is isomorphous with crystalline silicon?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals can be extracted using carbon as reducing agent?
questions.solution:
To determine which metal can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent, we need to consider the reactivity of the metals. Carbon is typically used to reduce the oxides of metals that are less reactive than carbon itself in the reactivity series. Here's a brief explanation for each metal mentioned in the options:

Option A: Zinc

Zinc is generally extracted from its ore using carbon. The chemical equation for the reduction of zinc oxide by carbon can be written as:

$$ \text{ZnO} + \text{C} \rightarrow \text{Zn} + \text{CO} $$

Here, carbon acts as a reducing agent, converting zinc oxide (ZnO) to zinc (Zn).

Option B: Silver

Silver is usually extracted by other methods such as the amalgamation process or cyanide process rather than using carbon. This is because silver is already quite unreactive and exists in nature mostly in its elemental form or in ores that do not require reduction by carbon.

Option C: Gold

Gold is even more unreactive than silver and is usually found in its native state. Therefore, it is not extracted using carbon. Instead, processes like cyanidation are employed to extract gold from its ores.

Option D: Aluminum

Aluminum is a highly reactive metal and cannot be extracted by reduction with carbon. Instead, it is extracted using electrolysis of aluminum oxide in the Hall-Héroult process.

Based on this analysis, the metal that can be extracted using carbon as a reducing agent is:

Option A: Zinc

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a heterogeneous mixture ?
options: Mixture of sugar 8 and salt crystals
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

Step-by-step:

A heterogeneous mixture has non-uniform composition, and its different parts can be seen separately.

Now check each option:

  1. Sulphur in carbon disulphide

    Sulphur dissolves completely in carbon disulphide, so it forms a homogeneous mixture, not a heterogeneous mixture.

  2. Mixture of sugar and salt crystals

    Sugar crystals and salt crystals remain separate, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  3. Sandy water

    Sand does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  4. Sulphur in water

    Sulphur does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

Therefore, the one which is not a heterogeneous mixture is:

$$\boxed{\text{Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
Match List-I with List-II and select the correct answer using the code given below the Lists:
List-I
(Allotrope of Carbon)		 List-II
(Property)
A. Graphite 1. Thinnest and strongest material
B. Diamond 2. Hardest natural substance
C. Fullerene 3. Very light and strong material
D. Graphene 4. Soft and slippery material
questions.solution:
To match the items in List-I (Allotrope of Carbon) with their respective properties in List-II, let's analyze each allotrope and their known properties:

Graphite: Graphite is known for being a soft and slippery material. It is often used as a lubricant and in pencils due to its layers that can easily slide over each other.

Diamond: Diamond is renowned for being the hardest natural substance. It has a high degree of hardness and is commonly used in cutting tools and as gemstones.

Fullerene: Fullerenes are carbon structures known for being very light and strong. They have unique properties that have potential applications in nanotechnology and materials science.

Graphene: Graphene is widely recognized as the thinnest and strongest material. It is a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice and has extraordinary electrical, thermal, and mechanical properties.

Based on the properties described for each allotrope, the correct matching of List-I to List-II should be:

List-I
(Allotrope of Carbon)		 List-II
(Property)		 Correct Matching
A. Graphite 4. Soft and slippery material 4
B. Diamond 2. Hardest natural substance 2
C. Fullerene 3. Very light and strong material 3
D. Graphene 1. Thinnest and strongest material 1

Thus, the correct option is:

A B C D
4 2 3 1

Therefore, the correct answer is Option A.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following nitrogen oxides may dimerize?
questions.solution:
The oxide that dimerizes is NO₂. Here’s why:

NO₂ is a free radical (it has an unpaired electron).

Two NO₂ molecules can pair up that unpaired electron to form dinitrogen tetroxide, N₂O₄.

The equilibrium is:

$$2\,NO_2 \;\rightleftharpoons\; N_2O_4$$

At lower temperatures the equilibrium shifts toward N₂O₄ (the dimer), while at higher temperatures it shifts back to NO₂.

None of the other oxides listed (N₂O, N₂O₃, N₂O₅) form such dimers under normal conditions.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about dihydrogen (H2) is not correct?
options: H<sub>2</sub> is lighter than air and insoluble in water.
H<sub>2</sub> is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy.
H<sub>2</sub> reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides.
A mixture of NO<sub>2</sub> and H<sub>2</sub> is known as Syngas.
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each statement regarding dihydrogen (H₂):

Option A: "H₂ is lighter than air and insoluble in water."

Correct. Hydrogen is indeed lighter than air and has very low solubility in water.

Option B: "H₂ is inert at room temperature due to high H-H bond dissociation enthalpy."

Correct. The H-H bond in H₂ is very strong (with a high dissociation enthalpy, approximately 436 kJ/mol), which makes the molecule relatively unreactive (or inert) under standard conditions unless a catalyst or extreme conditions are provided.

Option C: "H₂ reacts with alkali metals at high temperature to yield metal hydrides."

Correct. Alkali metals can react with hydrogen gas at elevated temperatures to form corresponding metal hydrides (e.g., sodium hydride, NaH).

Option D: "A mixture of NO₂ and H₂ is known as Syngas."

Not Correct. Syngas, short for synthesis gas, is typically a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). A mixture of nitrogen dioxide (NO₂) and H₂ does not constitute syngas.

Therefore, the statement that is not correct is Option D.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Galvanization is a method of protecting iron from rusting by coating with a thin layer of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Zinc. Here's why:

Galvanization is a process where a thin layer of zinc is applied to iron or steel to prevent rust. This protection works due to the following principles:

  • Sacrificial Protection: Zinc is more reactive than iron. When exposed to the environment, zinc will oxidize (react with oxygen) before the iron. This means the zinc layer acts as a sacrificial anode, corroding instead of the iron, thus protecting the underlying metal.
  • Barrier Protection: The zinc coating forms a physical barrier that prevents oxygen and moisture from reaching the iron surface, which are essential for rust formation.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: Gallium: Gallium is a metal, but it's not commonly used for galvanization. It's primarily used in electronics and other specialized applications.
  • Option B: Aluminum: Aluminum can also provide some protection against corrosion, but it's not as effective as zinc for galvanization. Aluminum forms a very thin oxide layer that can be easily damaged, making it less protective than a zinc coating.
  • Option D: Silver: Silver is a noble metal, meaning it's resistant to corrosion. However, it's too expensive and not as effective as zinc for protecting iron in everyday applications.

In summary, zinc is the most effective and widely used metal for galvanization due to its sacrificial and barrier protection properties. This makes it the best choice for protecting iron from rust.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following type of medicines is used for treating indigestion ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 4: Antacid

Explanation:

Indigestion is usually caused by excess acid in the stomach. Antacids are medicines that help to neutralise the extra acid and give relief from indigestion.

Other options:

  • Analgesic: used to relieve pain

  • Antiseptic: used to prevent infection in wounds

  • Antibiotic: used to kill or stop the growth of bacteria

So, the medicine used for treating indigestion is Antacid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: C<sub>4</sub>H<sub>8</sub> belongs to the homologous series of
questions.solution:
To determine the homologous series to which the compound C4H8 belongs, we should first recognize that the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and cycloalkenes vary and can be used to classify the compound.

The general formulas are:

Alkanes: $$C_nH_{2n+2}$$

Alkenes: $$C_nH_{2n}$$

Alkynes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Cycloalkenes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Here, the compound C4H8 follows the formula for alkenes:

$$C_4H_8 \implies C_nH_{2n} \text{ where } n = 4$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Alkenes
